statements.impact_statement: Medium-severity finding in Zope's AccessControl 4.2. Pending principal review of Plone 5.2.4's role-mapping path; plonehotfix20200121 is applied (per buildout.cfg) but does not cover this advisory. No deployment-specific not_affected reasoning yet.
Medium-severity finding in AccessControl 4.2. Pending principal review of role-evaluation path under Plone 5.2.4.
Medium-severity finding in AccessControl 4.2. Pending principal review.

Medium-severity ajv finding in a transitive npm dep of Plone's resource registry build. Pending confirmation that the bundled output of Plone 5.2.4's resource registry actually includes ajv runtime code (vs. a build-time-only dep) before promoting to not_affected.
Medium-severity ajv finding. Same context as GHSA-2g4f-4pwh-qvx6: pending verification of ajv runtime presence in Plone's compiled resource bundle.

Medium-severity Bootstrap 3 XSS in tooltip/popover. Bootstrap 3 is upstream EOL; SENAITE 2.0.0's UI inherits Plone 5.2.4's bootstrap dependency. Pending principal review of which Bootstrap 3 components are actually invoked in SENAITE templates and whether the tooltip vector is reachable from authenticated lab-staff input.
Medium-severity Bootstrap 3 finding. Same context: pending review of SENAITE's actual Bootstrap component usage.

High-severity cairosvg SSRF/XXE-class finding. CairoSVG is pulled in as a WeasyPrint dependency for PDF rendering of SENAITE reports/labels. Server-side SVG rendering against authenticated-only LIMS report data is the relevant deployment path; pending principal review of whether report templates can embed externally-fetched URI references and the CairoSVG version's url_fetcher configuration. Defaulting to under_investigation rather than not_affected.
High-severity cairosvg finding. Same review context as GHSA-f38f-5xpm-9r7c: WeasyPrint-driven report rendering needs principal review of url_fetcher / external-resource policy.
High-severity cairosvg finding. Same review context as the other cairosvg advisories.

statements.status_notes: certifi 2021.10.8 ships as the requests/urllib3 trust store. The advisory covers the e-Tugra root being included in the bundle. SENAITE LIMS deployments only make outbound TLS connections to operator-configured endpoints (admin sets the URL when configuring senaite.jsonapi/REST integrations), and the runtime never receives connections from arbitrary remote TLS servers — there is no path through which an attacker controls the server presenting an e-Tugra-issued certificate to senaite-lims. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot upgrade certifi past 2021.10.8 (requests 2.27.1's last 2.7-compatible pin); operators who do enable certifi-using outbound calls should verify their configured endpoint roots.
Same reasoning as GHSA-xqr8-7jwr-rhp7: certifi's trust store is consulted only for operator-configured outbound TLS endpoints; no attacker controls the server side.
Low-severity certifi root-CA finding; same deployment-context reasoning as the other certifi entries.

statements.impact_statement: High-severity datatables.net XSS in the browser-side rendering of cell content. SENAITE uses senaite.app.listing which renders datatables for authenticated lab staff; cell content is sourced from the LIMS database (admin-curated catalogs). Pending principal review of whether any cell-rendering pathway in senaite.app.listing reaches an unsanitised data column originating from less-trusted upload paths.
Medium-severity datatables.net finding. Same review context as GHSA-m7j4-fhg6-xf5v.

statements.status_notes: form-data 2.1.4 is a Node.js multipart-form library transitively pulled into Plone's resource registry build (via grunt/webpack tooling). The senaite-lims runtime image is a Plone/Zope server (bin/instance) — there is no Node.js runtime in the image, and the resource registry's compiled JS bundle that ships to authenticated browsers does not include form-data's HTTP-client code path (form-data is a server-side multipart constructor). The vulnerable random-boundary code never executes against the runtime.

statements.impact_statement: High-severity finding in `future` (Python 2/3 compatibility shim). future is a transitive dep of several SENAITE/Plone packages. The advisory covers a parsing path; pending principal review to enumerate which future-supplied modules SENAITE actually imports and whether any of those execute against untrusted input.

statements.status_notes: hawk 3.1.3 is a Node.js HTTP authentication library transitively pulled into Plone's resource-registry build via npm tooling. The senaite-lims runtime is a Plone/Zope server — there is no Node.js runtime in the image, and Plone's static asset bundle does not invoke hawk's auth code in the browser. The vulnerable code never executes against the deployed image.

hoek 2.16.3 is a Node.js utility library transitively present in Plone's resource-registry npm tree. The senaite-lims runtime has no Node.js interpreter — the only npm artefacts shipped are minified bundles served to authenticated browsers, and hoek's prototype-pollution surface is not exercised by Plone's bundle code paths. The vulnerable code never executes.
Same reasoning as GHSA-c429-5p7v-vgjp: hoek 2.16.3 is a transitive build-time npm dep with no runtime execution path in the Plone/Zope server.

statements.impact_statement: Medium-severity idna 2.10 finding. idna is consumed transitively by requests/urllib3 for hostname encoding. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot move past idna 2.10 (last 2.7-compatible release per buildout.cfg). Pending principal review of whether SENAITE accepts hostnames from untrusted callers.

Medium-severity jQuery 1.12.4 finding. jQuery is bundled via Plone's resource registry and runs browser-side for authenticated lab staff. Pending principal review of $.html()/parseHTML usage in SENAITE templates and whether any path passes attacker-controlled HTML.
Medium-severity jQuery 1.12.4 finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity jQuery 1.12.4 finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity jQuery 1.12.4 finding. Same review context.

statements.status_notes: json-schema 0.2.3 (npm) is a transitive build-time dep in Plone's resource registry. The vulnerability is a prototype-pollution path triggered when the library validates an attacker-crafted schema. The senaite-lims runtime image has no Node.js interpreter; Plone's compiled browser bundle does not feed user-supplied JSON schemas through this validator at runtime. The vulnerable code never executes.

statements.impact_statement: High-severity lxml XSS-via-cleaner finding. lxml is used by Plone for XML parsing and by senaite.impress for report rendering. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot move past lxml 4.6.x (the last 2.7-compatible series; pinned via Plone 5.2.4 versions). Pending principal review of whether SENAITE invokes lxml's HTML cleaner against untrusted input (TAL templates render server-side from trusted data, so the attack surface is narrow but needs confirmation).
Medium-severity lxml finding. Same review context as GHSA-vfmq-68hx-4jfw.
Medium-severity lxml finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity lxml finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity Python markdown finding. Pending principal review of which SENAITE/Plone code paths render markdown and whether they receive untrusted input.

High-severity marked.js ReDoS/XSS finding. marked 0.3.5 is bundled in Plone's resource registry and may render markdown in browser-side contexts where authenticated lab staff view comments / report annotations. Pending principal review of where SENAITE templates invoke marked browser-side and whether content originates from authenticated user input vs. admin-curated catalog data.
High-severity marked.js finding. Same review context as GHSA-5v2h-r2cx-5xgj.
High-severity marked.js finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity marked.js finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity marked.js finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity marked.js finding. Same review context.

statements.status_notes: minimist 1.2.5 is a Node.js argv-parser used by build-time tooling (grunt/webpack) in Plone's resource-registry pipeline. The senaite-lims runtime image has no Node.js interpreter and no shell to invoke a Node CLI; minimist's prototype-pollution surface is reachable only when minimist parses an attacker-controlled argv string, which never happens at runtime.

moment 2.24.0 is bundled into Plone's browser-side resource registry. The advisory covers a ReDoS in moment.parsing when the input string is attacker-controlled. SENAITE's UI passes only ISO-8601-formatted timestamps generated by the LIMS backend (sample dates, analysis timestamps) to moment(); the backend never echoes user-supplied date strings unsanitised into a moment() call. The ReDoS surface receives only well-formed timestamps.
Same reasoning as GHSA-8hfj-j24r-96c4: moment receives only LIMS-backend-generated timestamps, never raw user input.

statements.impact_statement: Critical Pillow 6.2.2 image-parsing finding. Pillow is the server-side image processor for sample uploads (microscopy, gels) by authenticated lab technicians. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot move past Pillow 6.2.2 (last 2.7-compatible release per buildout.cfg). The upload pipeline accepts files from authenticated users, so the attack surface is real. PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING until the upload pipeline's MIME whitelist, size cap, and processing-isolation strategy are documented.

Critical Pillow 6.2.2 finding. Same review context as GHSA-3f63-hfp8-52jq: server-side image processing on authenticated uploads is the attack surface; principal review of upload pipeline required before any not_affected call.

statements.status_notes: pip 20.3.4 is inherited from the python-2.7 base image (last 2.7-compatible release per dhi.yaml) and is also invoked at senaite-lims build time to pre-install transitive deps that have moved to Python 3 only (per prod.yaml `install zc.buildout` step). At runtime, the senaite-lims entrypoint is /opt/senaite/bin/instance (Plone Zope2 server); the image filesystem is read-only and there is no scheduler or shell that exec's pip. The vulnerable code path never fires on a deployed container. See python-2.7.vex.json for the same finding at the lower layer.
Same reasoning as wm-senaite-lims-pip-GHSA-5xp3-jfq3-5q8x: pip is build-time-only on the runtime image.
Same reasoning: pip is on disk but never exec'd at runtime.
Same reasoning: pip is build-time-only.
Same reasoning: pip is build-time-only.

statements.impact_statement: Critical Plone 5.2.4 advisory. The buildout pins Products.PloneHotfix20200121 = 1.1, which addresses Plone 5.2.x security issues from that hotfix series. Pending principal review of whether THIS advisory's CVE is covered by that hotfix or by a later hotfix that is not currently included. PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING.
High-severity Plone 5.2.4 finding. Same review context: confirm hotfix coverage.

Low-severity plone.namedfile finding. Pending principal review of file-upload pipeline (related to the broader Pillow review).
Medium-severity Products.ATContentTypes finding. Pending principal review of legacy Archetypes content surface in SENAITE.

High-severity Products.CMFCore finding. CMFCore is the foundational Plone content-management framework used everywhere in SENAITE. Pending principal review of the specific permission-check path the advisory covers.
Medium-severity Products.CMFCore finding (advisory shared with the plone GHSA-35rg-466w-77h3 row). Pending principal review.

High-severity Products.isURLInPortal finding (open-redirect class). The library is used by Plone to validate redirect targets after login/logout flows. SENAITE inherits Plone's auth flow; pending principal review of which redirect entry points are reachable by anonymous users vs. authenticated lab staff.
Medium-severity Products.isURLInPortal finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity PluggableAuthService finding (shared CVE id with the plone and products-cmfcore rows). Pending principal review.

High-severity PyJWT 1.7.1 finding. PyJWT is consumed by senaite.jsonapi for JWT-based REST authentication. Pending principal review of whether SENAITE's deployment enables senaite.jsonapi (it is opt-in via /@@API endpoints) and whether JWT signature-verification is configured against attacker-supplied algorithm headers.
High-severity PyJWT 1.7.1 finding. Same review context as GHSA-752w-5fwx-jx9f.

statements.status_notes: qs 6.4.0 is a Node.js querystring parser pulled in transitively by Plone's resource-registry npm tree. The senaite-lims runtime is a Plone/Zope server with no Node.js interpreter; the qs prototype-pollution path requires server-side qs.parse() of attacker-controlled input, which never happens at runtime. Plone's bin/instance uses Zope's own request parsing, not qs.
Same reasoning as GHSA-hrpp-h998-j3pp: qs is a build-time npm dep with no runtime execution path.

request 2.81.0 (npm) is a deprecated Node.js HTTP client transitively present in Plone's resource-registry npm tree. The senaite-lims runtime has no Node.js interpreter; the SSRF/auth-leak surface in request requires server-side execution which never happens on the deployed image.

statements.impact_statement: Medium-severity Python requests finding. requests 2.27.1 is the last 2.7-compatible release (pinned by buildout.cfg). Pending principal review of which SENAITE outbound calls go through requests and whether attacker-controlled URLs/headers reach them.
Medium-severity Python requests finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity Python requests finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity Python requests finding. Same review context.

statements.status_notes: requirejs 2.3.6 is the AMD module loader bundled into Plone's resource-registry browser bundle. The advisory covers a path-handling bug exploitable when requirejs loads attacker-controlled module paths. SENAITE's UI loads only resource-registry-precompiled modules whose paths are baked at build time; there is no runtime API that allows an authenticated user (let alone an anonymous one) to ask requirejs to fetch a custom module path.

statements.impact_statement: High-severity RestrictedPython sandbox-escape finding. RestrictedPython is Plone's TAL/Through-The-Web sandbox; SENAITE allows Manager-role users (admins) to author TTW Python scripts via Plone's Script (Python) content type. The exploit path requires Manager role, which is a trusted role per Plone's ACL — but sandbox escapes from Manager are still security-relevant if other roles can trigger Manager-authored scripts. PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING.
High-severity RestrictedPython finding. Same review context as GHSA-5rfv-66g4-jr8h.
High-severity RestrictedPython finding. Same review context.

Medium-severity Select2 3.5.4 finding (XSS class). Select2 is bundled in Plone's UI for select-widget rendering. Pending principal review of where SENAITE templates feed user-derived content into select2 options (vs. admin-curated catalog values).

statements.status_notes: setuptools 44.1.1 is the last 2.7-compatible release (pinned by buildout.cfg); it is inherited from the python-2.7 base image and used at senaite-lims build time only. The runtime image entrypoint is /opt/senaite/bin/instance; setuptools is never invoked at runtime. See python-2.7.vex.json for the same finding at the lower layer.
Same reasoning: setuptools is build-time-only on the runtime image.
Same reasoning.

statements.impact_statement: High-severity TinyMCE 4.6.2 XSS finding. TinyMCE is the rich-text editor bundled in Plone's resource registry. SENAITE uses TinyMCE in admin/content-edit forms accessible to lab staff with editor permissions. Pending principal review of XSS reachability when an editor user pastes attacker-crafted HTML into a field that another user later renders.

statements.status_notes: tough-cookie 2.3.4 is a Node.js cookie-jar library transitively present in Plone's resource-registry npm tree. The senaite-lims runtime has no Node.js interpreter; the prototype-pollution surface in tough-cookie is server-side and never executes against the deployed image.

Critical underscore.js _.template arbitrary-code-execution finding. Exploitation requires the application to call _.template() with an attacker-controlled template string. SENAITE's UI uses underscore _.template() only with templates that are statically defined in the Plone resource registry (compiled at build time from senaite/plone source); there is no code path in which a user-supplied string is passed to _.template(). The deployment never feeds adversary-controlled input into the vulnerable function.
Same reasoning as GHSA-cf4h-3jhx-xvhq: _.template templates are build-time-static in SENAITE's Plone resource registry, never user-supplied.

statements.impact_statement: High-severity urllib3 1.26.18 finding. urllib3 1.26.18 is the last 2.7-compatible release (pinned by buildout.cfg). urllib3 underlies requests for outbound HTTP. Pending principal review of which SENAITE outbound calls reach urllib3 with attacker-derived URLs/headers.
High-severity urllib3 1.26.18 finding. Same review context.
High-severity urllib3 1.26.18 finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity urllib3 1.26.18 finding. Same review context.
Medium-severity urllib3 1.26.18 finding. Same review context.

statements.status_notes: uuid 3.4.0 (npm) is a transitive build-time dep in Plone's resource-registry npm tree. The senaite-lims runtime has no Node.js interpreter; the npm uuid package is not the same as Python's `uuid` module and never executes on the deployed image.

waitress is a WSGI server; SENAITE 2.0.0's buildout sets `wsgi = off` for the Plone instance (per prod.yaml's BUILDOUT_CFG: `[instance] ... wsgi = off`). The runtime entrypoint is /opt/senaite/bin/instance, which uses Zope's classic ZServer, not waitress. waitress is in the SBOM as a transitive dep but its HTTP-serving code path is never invoked at runtime.
Same reasoning as GHSA-3f84-rpwh-47g6: waitress's HTTP server is not invoked because `wsgi = off` selects the ZServer entrypoint instead.

statements.impact_statement: High-severity WeasyPrint 0.42.3 finding (SSRF/HTML-fetch class). WeasyPrint generates PDF reports/labels for SENAITE; the rendering path includes URL fetching for CSS/images. Pending principal review of url_fetcher configuration and which SENAITE report templates can embed externally-fetched URI references. Tightly coupled to the cairosvg findings since both are part of the same PDF rendering pipeline.

Medium-severity WebOb 1.8.6 finding. WebOb is a transitive dep used by waitress / various WSGI middlewares. Since `wsgi = off` selects the ZServer path in our deployment, WebOb's request-parsing surface is likely unreachable; pending principal confirmation.

statements.status_notes: werkzeug 1.0.1 is a Flask-stack WSGI utility and is in SENAITE's buildout dep tree only as a transitive dep. SENAITE 2.0.0 runs on Zope/Plone (bin/instance), not Flask; werkzeug's HTTP request/response objects are never instantiated at runtime. The buildout pins `wsgi = off`, so even WSGI-bridged paths that might import werkzeug are not invoked.
Same reasoning as GHSA-2g68-c3qc-8985: werkzeug's HTTP code path is never invoked under SENAITE's Zope/ZServer runtime.

wheel 0.33.6 is inherited from the python-2.7 base image (last 2.7-compatible release). It is invoked at build time via pip install but never at runtime — the senaite-lims entrypoint is /opt/senaite/bin/instance. See python-2.7.vex.json for the same finding at the lower layer.

statements.impact_statement: Medium-severity zipp 1.2.0 finding. zipp is a backport library used by importlib_metadata. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot move past zipp 1.2.0 (pinned in buildout.cfg). Pending principal review of which SENAITE imports trigger zipp's vulnerable path.

High-severity Zope 4.5.5 finding. Zope is the application server SENAITE runs on. Pending principal review of the specific subsystem the advisory covers and whether the Plone hotfix series addresses it.
